sections.paragraph:
νῦν [Skt. assoc.]

—1. marker of time in the present, now Mt 27:42f; Mk 10:30; J 2:8; 12:27; Ro 5:9; emphatically just now J 11:8; Ac 7:52; Phil 1:20. As adj. Ro 3:26; 11:5. As noun τὸ νῦν Mt 24:21; Lk 1:48; 5:10; Ac 18:6; Ro 8:22; Phil 1:5. As adv. τὰ νῦν Ac 4:29; 17:30; 27:22; cp. τὸ νῦν ἔχον for the present time 24:25.

—2. marker with less temporal emphasis and with focus rather on implications posed by consideration of matters in the relatively present moment as the matter now stands, now J 18:36; Ac 15:10; 1 Th 3:8. In contrasting statement νῦν δέ but now, but as it is, as a matter of fact Lk 19:42; J 8:40; 18:36; 1 Cor 5:11; 12:20; Hb 11:16; Js 4:16.
